title: Q&A: How KRAFTON Built PUBG Ally, a Co-Playable Character Powered by NVIDIA ACE | NVIDIA Technical Blog
gist: KRAFTON built PUBG Ally, a co-playable character for PUBG: BATTLEGROUNDS, using NVIDIA ACE with a 2B-parameter on-device small language model to achieve real-time AI companionship in a live battle royale environment. The system architecture separates fast reflexive actions (behavior tree) from slower language reasoning (SLM) using a System 1/System 2 design pattern, and supports English, Korean, and Chinese with structured long- and short-term memory. The public beta launched June 17, 2026 in PUBG Arcade Mode, representing a new product category KRAFTON calls the co-playable character (CPC).

themes.theme: NVIDIA ACE as On-Device AI Gaming Platform

insights.claim: NVIDIA ACE enables a full AI companion pipeline (ASR + 2B-parameter SLM + TTS) to run entirely on-device in a live battle royale game, targeting GPUs with as little as 8GB VRAM, which demonstrates the commercial viability of edge AI inference for AAA gaming titles.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: NVIDIA ACE is proving traction as an on-device AI platform for gaming, extending its addressable market to all RTX GPU owners rather than requiring cloud inference infrastructure.
insights.quote: Running a NVIDIA ACE Mistral-NeMo-Minitron-2B SLM locally removed the network round-trip and gave us far more predictable response times during gameplay.
insights.locator: Why a small language model rather than a larger LLM?

insights.claim: KRAFTON chose on-device inference over cloud-hosted LLMs for PUBG Ally because network latency combined with model inference latency made cloud responses feel too slow for live squad communication in user testing, validating on-device AI as a technical requirement rather than a cost-optimization choice for real-time gaming.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Cloud AI providers face a structural disadvantage in real-time gaming applications; on-device inference vendors like NVIDIA gain a durable competitive moat in this growing segment.
insights.quote: When we tested cloud-hosted LLM approaches, the combination of network latency and model inference latency often made responses feel too slow for live squad communication.
insights.locator: Why a small language model rather than a larger LLM?

insights.claim: Quantizing the Mistral-NeMo-Minitron-2B model for client-side deployment allowed PUBG Ally to run within the VRAM headroom remaining after PUBG's GPU memory consumption, targeting a minimum of 8GB total VRAM, which defines the minimum hardware specification for NVIDIA ACE-powered AI companions.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Quantization of 2B-parameter models to fit within 8GB VRAM defines the mainstream RTX GPU tier as the addressable install base for real-time AI gaming companions.
insights.quote: The quantized model fits within the VRAM headroom that remains after PUBG, allowing PUBG Ally to run on GPUs with as little as 8GB of total VRAM.
insights.locator: Why a small language model rather than a larger LLM?

themes.theme: Co-Playable Character (CPC) as New Game Product Category

insights.claim: KRAFTON is defining a new game character category called the co-playable character (CPC), distinct from NPCs, characterized by real-time voice interaction, dynamic adaptation to game state, and persistent cross-session memory — signaling a potential shift in how game studios categorize and monetize AI companion features.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: The CPC category, if it gains industry adoption, could create new licensing, subscription, or premium content revenue streams for game studios deploying AI companions.
insights.quote: KRAFTON is calling the result a co-playable character (CPC): a new category distinct from non-playable characters (NPCs), designed to cooperate, adapt, and remember across sessions.
insights.locator: Introduction

insights.claim: PUBG: BATTLEGROUNDS' battle royale format — with its emphasis on teamwork, unpredictable emergent situations, and tactical real-time decisions — was selected as the optimal genre to introduce CPCs because it maximizes the meaningful cooperation scenarios where AI teammates provide differentiated value over scripted NPCs.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Battle royale and co-op multiplayer genres represent the highest-value initial market for AI companion technology, informing which game categories studios should prioritize for AI integration investments.
insights.quote: PUBG: BATTLEGROUNDS was the ideal title to introduce this concept because PUBG's gameplay is heavily driven by teamwork, communication, and unpredictable emergent situations.
insights.locator: KRAFTON has framed PUBG Ally as a co-playable character...

insights.claim: PUBG Ally entered public beta on June 17, 2026 in PUBG Arcade Mode with availability through June 30, making it the first commercial-scale deployment of an NVIDIA ACE-powered CPC in a live AAA title.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: The beta timeline provides a near-term data point on player adoption and retention of AI companions in live-service games, with results likely informing NVIDIA's ACE platform marketing and KRAFTON's product roadmap.
insights.quote: PUBG Ally entered public beta on June 17 and will be available in PUBG: BATTLEGROUNDS Arcade Mode for gamers until June 30th.
insights.locator: Introduction

themes.theme: System 1/System 2 AI Architecture for Real-Time Games

insights.claim: KRAFTON implemented a dual-layer AI architecture for PUBG Ally where a behavior tree handles fast reflexive gameplay actions (movement, aiming, combat reactions at game tick rate) while the 2B-parameter SLM handles deliberate reasoning and speech generation, preventing latency-sensitive actions from being blocked by language model inference.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: The System 1/System 2 architecture pattern is likely to become the industry standard for real-time AI game agents, with significant engineering effort required to define the boundary between the two layers.
insights.quote: A System 1 layer, implemented as a behavior tree, handles fast, reactive gameplay such as movement, aiming, and immediate combat responses at game tick rate. A System 2 layer, the language model, handles the deliberate work: interpreting player intent, coordinating with the player, and generating natural speech.
insights.locator: Latency is everything in a battle royale.

insights.claim: Defining the boundary between the fast behavior-tree layer and the slow language-model layer in PUBG Ally was identified as the most significant engineering effort, underscoring that the System 1/System 2 interface design is the primary technical challenge in real-time AI agent development for games.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Game studios attempting to build real-time AI companions will find the action-boundary design problem to be a recurring bottleneck, creating demand for NVIDIA ACE tooling or third-party frameworks that help developers partition agent logic.
insights.quote: A significant amount of engineering effort went into defining the boundary between those two layers, deciding what should be handled immediately by the System 1 gameplay layer and what should be routed through the System 2 language model.
insights.locator: Latency is everything in a battle royale.

insights.claim: KRAFTON structured PUBG Ally's prompts to maximize KV cache reuse across turns by keeping stable instructions and gameplay context consistent while only updating dynamic real-time information, achieving more predictable on-device response times — a technique that can significantly reduce inference costs and latency for SLMs in production game deployments.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Prompt engineering for KV cache stability is an under-discussed lever for reducing on-device inference latency and compute cost in real-time AI gaming applications.
insights.quote: We designed the prompt structure to make the best use of the KV cache. Stable instructions and gameplay context are kept as consistent as possible across turns, while only the most relevant real-time information is updated each turn.
insights.locator: Latency is everything in a battle royale.

insights.claim: KRAFTON used a teacher-student distillation pipeline where a larger teacher model was primed with a deterministic PUBG game specification (maps, weapon taxonomy, item rules) to generate grounded responses, which were then distilled into the on-device 2B-parameter student model — making domain knowledge intrinsic to the small model rather than relying on runtime retrieval.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Teacher-student distillation with domain-specific game specifications is an effective and scalable method for embedding game knowledge into small on-device models, reducing reliance on large RAG databases at inference time.
insights.quote: The teacher used it to produce grounded responses, demonstrating both the right PUBG terminology and style and its own limits, including when to call a knowledge-lookup tool backed by a curated dictionary of weapons, attachments, items, and rules. We distilled all of that into the on-device student model, so those behaviors became the student's own rather than a set of instructions.
insights.locator: PUBG Ally understands PUBG-specific terminology...

insights.claim: KRAFTON constrained PUBG Ally to a single map (Sanhok), single mode (AI Duo), and a fixed item taxonomy to make domain adaptation tractable, demonstrating that deliberate scope limitation rather than broad generalization is the recommended approach for initial AI companion deployments in complex live-service games.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Scope-constrained AI companion launches (one map, one mode) represent a lower-risk path to production for game studios than attempting full-game AI coverage, with lessons directly applicable to other live-service game operators.
insights.quote: Rather than asking the model to cope with every possible PUBG scenario, we constrained the world: a single map, Sanhok, and a single mode, AI Duo, with a fixed item taxonomy.
insights.locator: PUBG Ally understands PUBG-specific terminology...

insights.claim: PUBG Ally uses agentic tool calls to observation APIs (own status, teammate status, nearby items, combat situation) as the sole ground truth for live game state, with teacher-distilled behavior reinforcing that the model re-observes through tools rather than relying on earlier conversation turns — preventing hallucination of stale or fabricated game facts.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Real-time tool-call grounding is the key architectural element preventing factual drift in game AI companions, and this pattern is likely to become a standard requirement in production game AI systems.
insights.quote: The system prompt establishes that these tool results are the only ground truth for the current match, and during distillation the teacher reinforced the habit of re-observing through tools rather than recalling from earlier turns.
insights.locator: PUBG Ally understands PUBG-specific terminology...

insights.claim: PUBG Ally implements a two-tier memory architecture — long-term (cross-match player profile with name, preferred weapons, and drop location preferences) and short-term (within-match voice and event context) — and player testing confirmed that cross-session memory was the feature that transformed Ally from a per-match assistant into a persistent AI teammate.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Cross-session memory is the differentiating feature for AI companion retention and player relationship-building, suggesting game studios should prioritize persistent memory architecture over per-session intelligence improvements.
insights.quote: These were the moments that turned Ally from a per-match assistant into a teammate players treated as ongoing.
insights.locator: At CES 2026, KRAFTON showed PUBG Ally with long-term memory.

insights.claim: KRAFTON first demonstrated PUBG Ally with long-term memory capabilities at CES 2026, indicating the company has been developing the CPC concept for at least six months before the June 2026 public beta — suggesting a multi-year AI companion R&D investment cycle for AAA game studios.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: The CES 2026 to June 2026 timeline signals that AAA studios deploying AI companions require 12+ month development cycles, which favors established platform vendors like NVIDIA over startups in this market.
insights.locator: At CES 2026, KRAFTON showed PUBG Ally with long-term memory.

themes.theme: AI Companion Evaluation and Iteration Methodology

insights.claim: KRAFTON validated PUBG Ally through large-scale playtests with over a thousand real players, combining automated protocol-compliance evaluations, A/B tests, surveys, and free-form feedback — establishing that conventional offline metrics are insufficient for evaluating AI teammates and must be supplemented with large-scale live playtesting.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Game studios cannot rely on standard NLP evaluation benchmarks to tune AI companions; large-scale live playtesting infrastructure is a required capability, raising the barrier to entry for this product category.
insights.quote: We validated selected models through large-scale playtests, refining Ally with play feedback from over a thousand real players.
insights.locator: What did the iteration loop look like?

insights.claim: KRAFTON's core advice to studios building AI companions is to get a fast prototype in front of large numbers of real players early, run the feedback loop cheaply and often, because each iteration cycle taught more about what constitutes a good AI teammate than any upfront design effort — implying a lean, experimental product development philosophy for AI gaming companions.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Studios that can iterate rapidly on AI companion designs with real player feedback will build a durable advantage over those relying on internal quality metrics, making live-service game infrastructure increasingly important for AI product development.
insights.quote: The loop that mattered most was simple: develop a fast prototype, put it in front of a large number of real players, and improve the model from their feedback, then repeat.
insights.locator: What advice would you give to other studios...

insights.claim: PUBG Ally was presented in a GDC 2026 deep-dive session by KRAFTON Deep Learning engineers and NVIDIA experts, signaling that NVIDIA is actively co-marketing NVIDIA ACE with game studio partners at major industry events to accelerate platform adoption.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: NVIDIA's co-presentation strategy at GDC 2026 indicates active developer ecosystem building around NVIDIA ACE, a pattern consistent with how NVIDIA historically expanded CUDA adoption through developer partnerships.
insights.locator: Video 2: Watch the full replay of our GDC 2026 session...

themes.theme: Multilingual AI Companion Support in Global Games

insights.claim: PUBG Ally supports English, Korean, and Chinese at launch, with language-specific training data built from research into how PUBG players actually communicate in each language community (including slang, abbreviations, item names, map callouts) and reviewed by language experts — showing that multilingual AI companions require community-specific rather than purely translation-based localization approaches.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Multilingual AI game companion support requires investment in community-specific linguistics research, not just model translation, which raises localization costs and complexity relative to traditional game content localization.
insights.quote: We built language-specific data by researching how PUBG players actually communicate in English, Korean, and Chinese communities. We also worked with language experts to review quality, naturalness, and gameplay appropriateness across each language.
insights.locator: PUBG Ally supports English, Korean, and Chinese.

insights.claim: PUBG Ally uses a custom in-house TTS model for voice synthesis rather than a third-party TTS solution, suggesting KRAFTON is building proprietary AI voice capabilities alongside NVIDIA ACE's ASR and SLM components — indicating game studios are investing in owned AI voice assets as a differentiation strategy.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Game studios are beginning to develop proprietary TTS models for AI companions rather than relying entirely on platform vendors, which could fragment the AI voice market for gaming and reduce dependency on NVIDIA ACE's full stack.
insights.quote: NVIDIA ACE and custom AI models run on-device: NVIDIA automatic speech recognition (ASR), NVIDIA small language model (SLM), and a custom in-house text-to-speech (TTS) model.
insights.locator: Walk us through the technical architecture of PUBG Ally.

themes.theme: CPC Product Roadmap and Discovery Strategy

insights.claim: KRAFTON is using the PUBG Ally arcade mode beta primarily as a player behavior discovery exercise to learn which AI companion capabilities players gravitate toward before deciding what to build next, framing the beta as product research rather than a feature release — a 'discovery before roadmap' product development philosophy.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: KRAFTON's explicit discovery-first framing suggests that AI companion product-market fit is not yet established, implying the CPC category is still in early exploration rather than proven growth — investors should monitor retention and engagement data from the beta as leading indicators.
insights.quote: For us, the most important next step is discovery. Through the arcade mode, we want to learn how players actually want to enjoy the game when they have an AI feature like Ally playing alongside them, and use what we find to prepare genuinely new kinds of fun for the game.
insights.locator: Looking ahead, where do you see CPCs going next?

insights.claim: KRAFTON indicated multi-agent cooperation between multiple PUBG Ally instances and richer sensory inputs as potential future directions for CPC development, which would require significant increases in on-device compute and VRAM beyond the current 8GB minimum threshold.
insights.speaker: Phillip Singh
insights.so_what: Future multi-agent CPC architectures could drive demand for higher VRAM GPU tiers (16GB+), benefiting NVIDIA's RTX 5000 series positioning and creating an upgrade cycle tied to AI companion feature evolution.
insights.locator: Looking ahead, where do you see CPCs going next?
